lede: A look at July 30 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Joey King and Matthew Scott (rugby league), and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2025 Kamchatka earthquake
events.desc: A magnitude 8.8 earthquake hits Russia, causing tsunamis over the Pacific Ocean.

events.title: 2014 Malin landslide
events.desc: Twenty killed and 150 are trapped after a landslide in Maharashtra, India.

events.title: Nellore train fire
events.desc: A train fire kills 32 passengers and injures 27 on the Tamil Nadu Express in Andhra Pradesh, India.

events.title: Jimmy Hoffa
events.desc: Jimmy Hoffa disappears from the parking lot of the Machus Red Fox restaurant in Bloomfield Hills, Michigan, a suburb of Detroit, at about 2:30 p.m. He is never seen or heard from again.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Japanese submarine I-58 sinks the USS Indianapolis, killing 883 seamen. Most die during the following four days, until an aircraft notices the survivors.

events.title: Montevideo
events.desc: In Montevideo, Uruguay wins the first FIFA World Cup by beating Argentina.

events.title: Staten Island Ferry
events.desc: The Staten Island Ferry Westfield's boiler explodes, killing over 85 people.

events.title: Miguel Hidalgo y Costilla
events.desc: Father Miguel Hidalgo y Costilla, leader of the Mexican insurgency, is executed by the Spanish in Chihuahua City, Mexico.

events.title: Freemasonry
events.desc: The first Masonic Grand Lodge in the future United States is constituted in Massachusetts.

deaths.text: Miguel Hidalgo y Costilla, Mexican priest and soldier

observances.text: Christian feast day: Maxima, Donatilla, and Secunda
